prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate, or the edit/upscale instruction.

operation.description: Default 'generate'. 'edit' transforms input_image per the prompt; 'upscale' increases its resolution. edit/upscale REQUIRE input_image.

input_image.description: For edit/upscale: the source image. Accepts an https URL, a session upload id (from list_uploads), a local file path, a base64 string, or a "data:image/...;base64," URI. PREFER the upload id or file path for local images — read server-side, never inlined into your context.

model.description: Abacus image model id (free-form string — pass any id below). Defaults per operation: generate→flux_pro, edit→flux_kontext_edit, upscale→magnific. AVAILABLE MODELS — generate: flux_pro, flux_pro_ultra, flux2, flux2_pro, flux_kontext, flux_pro_canny, flux_pro_depth, gpt_image15, gpt_image2, imagen, nano_banana, nano_banana2, nano_banana_lite, nano_banana_pro, ideogram, ideogram_character, midjourney, seedream, recraft, recraft_svg, dreamina, hunyuan_image, imagine_art, grok_imagine_image, grok_imagine_image_quality. edit (need input_image): flux_kontext_edit, gpt_image_edit, gpt_image2_edit, qwen_image_edit, recraft_vectorize. upscale (need input_image): magnific. Known-UNSUPPORTED (will fail, do not use): dalle, wan27. Live list: /api/models?provider=abacus (media_type=image).

num_images.description: How many images to generate (1-4, generate only). Default 1.

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

model.description: Abacus TTS model id. Defaults to gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts (verified). Others: gpt-audio-mini, gemini-2.5-pro-preview-tts. Not every listed id is invocable (openai_tts/elevenlabs are rejected).

voice.description: Voice id/name, model-specific. Omit for the model default.

format.description: Audio output format, e.g. "mp3", "wav", "opus". Default mp3.

id.description: The image upload id from list_uploads.

question.description: Optional focus for the vision model.

note_id.description: Id of the note to append to (from list_notes). Preferred when known.

title.description: Title of the note to append to. If no note with this title exists it is created. Use this to keep building ONE continuous note across turns.

content.description: Markdown to append to the end of the note.

attribution.description: Insert a "— author · timestamp" divider before this block. Default true; set false to append seamlessly into one flowing document.

permanent.description: When true, permanently delete the MinIO object and DB row. Default false (soft-delete via archived flag).

message.description: The message or question to send to the agent team.

agent_name.description: Route directly to a named agent instead of Alfred. Must match the agent's name exactly (case-insensitive).

session_id.description: Continue an existing conversation session. Omit to start a fresh session.

to.description: Agent name to assign the task to.

execute_now.description: Run immediately and return the result.

agent.description: Agent name or id to configure.

enabled.description: Turn TTS on or off for this agent. When off, the speaker button is hidden in the dashboard and Discord skips the audio attachment.

provider.description: TTS backend. Defaults to voidai when first enabling. ElevenLabs requires ELEVENLABS_API_KEY; Kokoro requires KOKORO_API_KEY.

voice.description: Voice id for the chosen provider. VoidAI: alloy / echo / fable / onyx / nova / shimmer. ElevenLabs: a voice_id from audio_list_voices. Kokoro: a voice id from audio_list_voices with provider=kokoro. Pass empty string to clear and use the env default.

bot.description: Discord bot name or id (from discord_list_bots).

enabled.description: Master switch for whether this bot attaches synthesized .mp3s to its replies. Inbound voice transcription always works regardless of this flag.

provider.description: Filter to one provider. Omit to get all. ElevenLabs is empty unless ELEVENLABS_API_KEY is configured; Kokoro is empty unless KOKORO_API_KEY is configured.

agent.description: Optional agent name or id to inspect. When omitted, returns voice config for ALL agents that have TTS on, plus the voice toggle for every Discord bot.

action.description: start the autonomous Mission Control loop, stop it, or report status.

maxTasks.description: Override: max tasks to work before reporting back.

defaultAgentName.description: Fallback agent (by name) for tasks with no assignee.

secrets.description: Broker secret names to inject as environment variables for this command, scoped to you. Values are placed directly into the child process and never shown to you; command output is scrubbed of them.

purpose.description: Short reason the secrets are needed — recorded in the broker audit log.

url.description: Absolute URL to fetch. Browserless renders the page in a headless Chromium and returns the post-JS HTML. NOT a search — use this when you need the rendered page content.

wait_for.description: Either a CSS selector to wait for, or a number of milliseconds to delay before snapshotting the DOM. Useful for SPA pages that hydrate after load.

include_main_text.description: When true, run @mozilla/readability on the HTML and include {mainText, title, byline, length} alongside the raw html. Great for article extraction.

include_screenshot.description: When true, also call /screenshot for the same URL and include a base64 JPEG (full page, quality 70) in the response.

url.description: Absolute URL to render as PDF via hosted Chromium.
Absolute URL the script will run against. The page is loaded with networkidle2 before the script body executes.

script.description: Body of an async function executed in the Puppeteer Node context (NOT in the page DOM). The variables `page` and `context` are in scope; `context.url` is the URL above. Use `await page.$eval(...)` to read DOM. End with `return ...;` to return data.

return_value.description: When true (default), the value returned by the script is JSON-decoded and surfaced as `result`. When false, only ok/status are returned.

url.description: Absolute URL to screenshot using hosted Chromium. Returns a base64 image — NOT a search result.

full_page.description: Capture the full scrollable page. Default true.

viewport.description: Browser viewport in pixels. Defaults to browserless's 1920x1080.

project_id.description: Restrict to tasks in this project id.

feature.description: Restrict to tasks with this feature label.

url.description: URL to crawl and index into the knowledge base.

deep.description: Multi-page BFS crawl instead of a single page.

workflow_json.description: Complete n8n workflow JSON as a string — must include name, nodes, and connections fields

job_id.description: ID of the job to delete

channel_id.description: Discord channel id (right-click channel → Copy ID with Developer Mode on).

agent.description: Agent name or id to handle messages in this channel.

require_mention.description: When true, override any guild-level auto-reply for this specific channel — bot will only respond to @mentions here, even if the server is otherwise on auto-reply. Useful when multiple bots share a server.

auto_reply.description: When true, bot replies to all messages in this channel without an @mention, regardless of guild-level auto-reply settings. Useful for channels "owned" by a specific agent on a shared server.

channel_id.description: Discord channel id where the message lives.

emoji.description: Emoji to react with — unicode like "👍" "🔥" "❤️", or custom guild emoji as <:name:id>.

message_id.description: Specific message id to react to. Right-click message → Copy ID with Developer Mode on.

last_user_message.description: If true and message_id is omitted, react to the most recent non-bot message in the channel. Convenient for "react to what the user just said".

name.description: Display name for this bot configuration (e.g. "Coder Bot", "Team Server").

token.description: Discord bot token from the Developer Portal. Sensitive — never echo it back to the user.

default_agent.description: Agent name or id that handles mentions when no per-channel route matches.

application_id.description: Optional Discord application id (skips a startup REST call when set).

guild_ids.description: Array of Discord guild (server) ids where the bot should respond to every non-bot message without requiring an @mention. Empty array disables auto-reply everywhere.

bot_id.description: Discord bot id (from the system context — "bot_id" in your Discord context block).

user_id.description: Discord user id of the person whose preference you are setting (the "author id" in your context block).

enabled.description: Whether this user should receive .mp3 audio attachments on replies from this bot. Pass false when the user asks you to stop sending audio / voice / mp3s; pass true to re-enable.

reason.description: Short note for the audit log explaining why the toggle was flipped. Optional but recommended.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate, or the edit instruction to apply to input_image.

operation.description: Default 'generate' (text→image). 'edit' transforms input_image per the prompt and REQUIRES input_image.

input_image.description: For edit: the source image. fal's queue REQUIRES a public https URL — pass one directly, or pass a session upload id (from list_uploads), a local file path, or a base64/data: URI and the server will stage it to a public URL automatically. PREFER the upload id or file path for local images — read server-side, never inlined into your context. Ignored for generate.

image_size.description: fal image_size, e.g. "square_hd", "landscape_16_9", "portrait_4_3". Model-dependent. Generate only — edit models use aspect_ratio (baked into the prompt/model default) instead.

num_images.description: How many images to generate. Default 1.

model.description: fal model id (endpoint_id from fal's v1/models catalog). Default per operation: generate→"fal-ai/flux/schnell", edit→"fal-ai/nano-banana/edit". Others (generate, live-verified 2026-07-14): fal-ai/flux/dev, fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1, fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1-ultra, fal-ai/flux-2, fal-ai/flux-2-pro, fal-ai/nano-banana, fal-ai/nano-banana-2, fal-ai/nano-banana-pro, fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4/text-to-image, fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4.5/text-to-image, bytedance/seedream/v5/pro/text-to-image, fal-ai/ideogram/v3, fal-ai/recraft/v3/text-to-image, fal-ai/z-image/turbo, openai/gpt-image-2, xai/grok-imagine-image. Others (edit): fal-ai/nano-banana-2/edit, fal-ai/nano-banana-pro/edit, fal-ai/flux-pro/kontext, fal-ai/flux-pro/kontext/max, fal-ai/flux-2/edit, fal-ai/flux-2-pro/edit, fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4/edit, fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4.5/edit, bytedance/seedream/v5/pro/edit, fal-ai/gemini-25-flash-image/edit, fal-ai/gemini-3-pro-image-preview/edit, fal-ai/gpt-image-1.5/edit, openai/gpt-image-2/edit, xai/grok-imagine-image/edit.

safety_tolerance.description: Content filter strictness (strictest→most permissive). Only honored by the Nano-Banana family (default "4") and Pro-tier FLUX models like flux-pro/v1.1*, flux-2-pro/-max/-flex (default "2"). Silently ignored by models that don't expose it (flux/schnell, flux/dev, flux-2 base, gpt-image-*, seedream, ideogram, recraft, qwen-image, grok-imagine-image).

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

id.description: Archive item id to download locally.

dest_path.description: Optional absolute destination path. If omitted, writes to a persistent scratch path under _shared/archive-fetch/.

query.description: Keyword search across project title + description (case-insensitive).

project_id.description: Get a specific project by id; returns full record including docs/features/data.

include_archived.description: Include archived projects in the result. Default false.

task_id.description: Get a specific task by id (returns full record including sources + code_examples).

filter_by.description: Field to filter on. Pair with filter_value.

filter_value.description: Value for the chosen filter — status name, project id, assignee string, or parent task id.

project_id.description: Restrict to a single project (in addition to filter_by). Often used with filter_by=status.

oldString.description: Exact text to find. Must appear exactly once in the file.

prompt.description: Edit instruction to apply to the image.

image_path.description: Absolute path to a PNG or JPEG to edit.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate (Gemini Nano Banana / Imagen).

quality.description: "standard" uses grok-imagine-image (~5-10s). "hd" uses grok-imagine-image-quality (~10-20s). Defaults to standard.

templateHtml.description: Raw HTML template content containing Carbone tags like {d.name}. Optional if templatePath is provided.

templatePath.description: Absolute path to a local template file (DOCX, XLSX, ODT, ODS, HTML) on the host. Optional if templateHtml is provided.

data.description: JSON data payload representing the fields to merge into the template.

convertTo.description: The output document format. Default is pdf.

quality.description: "standard" uses grok-imagine-image (~5-10s). "hd" uses grok-imagine-image-quality (~10-20s). Defaults to standard.

prompt.description: What to generate. Be descriptive — Venice models respond well to detailed cinematic prompts.

model.description: Venice image model. Defaults to flux-2-pro. Other options: flux-dev, stable-diffusion-3-5, etc.

width.description: Output width in pixels. Defaults to 1024.

height.description: Output height in pixels. Defaults to 1024.

negative_prompt.description: What to avoid in the image (e.g. "blurry, low quality, watermark").

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

voice.description: xAI voice id override. Omit to use the default configured voice.

id.description: Archive item id from list_archive or register_archive.
The attachment_id surfaced in the system context block. NOT the filename — the UUID after "attachment_id:".
The attachment_id from the system context block — the UUID after "attachment_id:". Use this tool for files marked "✓ pre-parsed"; use get_attachment for the base64 fallback path.

hours.description: How many hours back to look (default 168 / 7 days).
How many hours back (default 24). Returns one bucket per hour.

job_id.description: Job ID to fetch run history for

hours.description: How many hours back to look (default 24, max 168).

task_id.description: The taskId returned by a prior run_subtask call

path.description: Base directory to search from. Defaults to the workspace root.

prompt.description: Edit instruction to apply to the image.

image_path.description: Absolute path to the image to edit.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate.
Instruction guiding how to composite/blend the images.

image_paths.description: Ordered absolute paths; referenced as "First image", "Second image", etc.

image_path.description: Absolute path to the image to edit.

quality.description: "standard" uses grok-imagine-image (~5-10s). "hd" uses grok-imagine-image-quality (~10-20s). Defaults to standard.

text.description: Raw text/markdown to index directly (no crawl).

label.description: Source label/id to group this content under.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate, or the edit instruction to apply to input_image.

operation.description: Default 'generate' (text→image). 'edit' transforms input_image per the prompt and REQUIRES input_image.

input_image.description: For edit: the source image. KIE's job queue REQUIRES a public https URL — pass one directly, or pass a session upload id (from list_uploads), a local file path, or a base64/data: URI and the server will stage it to a public URL automatically. PREFER the upload id or file path for local images — read server-side, never inlined into your context. Ignored for generate.

model.description: KIE media model id (unified createTask API). Default per operation: generate→"google/nano-banana", edit→"google/nano-banana-edit". AVAILABLE MODELS (live-verified 2026-07-14) — generate: google/nano-banana, google/imagen4, google/imagen4-fast, google/imagen4-ultra, bytedance/seedream, bytedance/seedream-v4-text-to-image, ideogram/v3-text-to-image, qwen/text-to-image, qwen2/text-to-image, grok-imagine/text-to-image. edit (need input_image): google/nano-banana-edit, bytedance/seedream-v4-edit, seedream/4.5-edit, seedream/5-lite-image-to-image, seedream/5-pro-image-to-image, ideogram/v3-edit, ideogram/v3-remix, ideogram/character-edit, qwen/image-edit, qwen/image-to-image, qwen2/image-edit, grok-imagine/image-to-image. NOTE: gpt-image and flux are NOT on KIE's unified API — use voidai_gpt_image / fal_image for those.

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

tag.description: Filter to items that include this exact tag.

pinned_first.description: Sort pinned items to the top. Default false (newest first).

status_filter.description: Filter by status group: 'active' = currently running, 'done' = completed/failed/blocked, 'all' = everything (default: 'all')

active.description: Filter to only active or inactive workflows. Omit to return all.

include_body.description: When true, return each skill's full markdown body. Default false (just metadata + scripts list).

pinned.description: Pin to top of project list.

hard.description: On delete: when true, permanently remove the row instead of archiving. Tasks are reassigned to the default NeuroClaw project. Cannot delete the default project.

docs.description: JSON array. Optional. Replaces existing docs.

features.description: JSON array. Optional. Replaces existing features.

data.description: JSON object. Optional. Replaces existing data.

action.description: "create" | "update" | "delete". Skills must live in the project (.claude/skills/) to be edited or deleted; user-global skills are read-only.

name.description: Skill name. On create: sanitized to lowercase + dashes. On update/delete: must match an existing skill.

description.description: One-line summary the agent's system prompt will display.

body.description: Markdown body — instructions to the agent on when/how to use the skill. Required on create.

triggers.description: Keywords or phrases that hint when the skill applies. Currently informational (no auto-routing).

tools.description: Allowed tool names — informational metadata for now.

scripts.description: Optional list of scripts to bundle on create. Each {filename, content} writes to scripts/<filename>. Use manage_skill_script to add later.

action.description: "create" | "update" | "delete". create and update use the same code path (writeSkillScript) — both overwrite if present.

content.description: Full file contents. Required on create/update.

action.description: "create" | "update" | "delete". Delete archives the task by default (use hard=true to remove).

project_id.description: Project id (required on create unless using default project).

description.description: Detailed task description with completion criteria.

status.description: Workflow status. todo→doing→review→done is the normal flow; use "blocked" to park a task that cannot proceed (needs input/dependency) or "cancelled" to abandon one you cannot or should not do — both cleanly release the task off the active board.

assignee.description: Free-text assignee. Accepts agent names, "User", "AI IDE Agent", or anything else. Defaults to "User" on create.

priority_level.description: Priority enum used by the Kanban UI.

task_order.description: Drag-reorder position within the (status) column. Lower numbers float up.

feature.description: Free-text feature label that cuts across projects (e.g. "auth", "billing").

sources.description: JSON array of citations. Each entry typically {url, title, relevance}. Hooks NeuroVault retrievals onto the task.

code_examples.description: JSON array of code snippet references. Each entry typically {file, line, summary}.

verification_mode.description: Dispatcher-only discriminator for the deterministic reconcile gate. 'reconcile' asserts main HEAD moved during the task; 'review' bypasses that assertion because a review is supposed to leave main untouched. Ignored on update — a task cannot mutate its own mode.

hard.description: On delete: when true, permanently remove. Default false (archive).

to.description: Name of the agent to message.

name.description: Exact tool name from search_tools results.

args_json.description: Tool arguments as a plain object (preferred) or a JSON string. Object example: {"skill_name":"humanize-text","script":"humanize.py","args":["your text here"]}. String example: '{"query":"test","limit":5}'. Pass {} or "{}" for tools with no required arguments. Use get_tool_schema(name) first if you are unsure of the parameter format.

name.description: Exact tool name to inspect (use search_tools to find it). Returns the full parameter schema so you know what to pass to call_tool.

query.description: Keywords describing what you need to do (2-6 words). Searched against tool names and descriptions.

notebook_id.description: Notebook id. If omitted, uses the active notebook for this conversation.

source.description: An uploaded document attachment_id, OR an https URL to a document (PDF/DOCX/HTML/MD/TXT). YouTube URLs are not supported in v1.

notebook_id.description: Notebook id. If omitted, uses the active notebook.

question.description: The question to answer using RAG across ALL documents in the notebook.

title.description: Name of the notebook (collection of documents).

description.description: Optional description of what the notebook is for.

notebook_id.description: Notebook id. If omitted, uses the active notebook.
The notebook id to make the active notebook for this conversation.
Notebook id. If omitted, uses the active notebook.

question.description: The question to answer using RAG across ALL documents in the notebook.

title.description: Name of the notebook (collection of documents).

description.description: Optional description of what the notebook is for.

notebook_id.description: Notebook id. If omitted, uses the active notebook for this conversation.

source.description: An uploaded document attachment_id, OR an https URL to a document (PDF/DOCX/HTML/MD/TXT). YouTube URLs are not supported in v1.

notebook_id.description: Notebook id. If omitted, uses the active notebook.
The notebook id to make the active notebook for this conversation.

message.description: The notification message to send to the user. Be clear and specific about what you need.

kind.description: Type of notification: info (general), question (needs user response/decision), alert (urgent/blocking issue), update (progress on long task).

context.description: Optional additional context, background info, or metadata to help the user understand the situation.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate, or the edit instruction to apply to input_image.

operation.description: Default 'generate' (text→image, mode text2image). 'edit' (mode image2image) transforms input_image per the prompt and REQUIRES input_image.

input_image.description: For edit: the source/reference image. Pass a session upload id (from list_uploads), a local file path, a base64/data: URI, or a public https URL — the server reads the bytes and uploads them to OpenArt via a presigned PUT. PREFER an upload id or file path for local images (read server-side, never inlined into your context). Ignored for generate.

model.description: OpenArt model id (NOT displayName). One of: nano-banana-2, nano-banana-pro, nano-banana-2-lite (default), gpt-image-2, byte-plus-seedream-4-5, byte-plus-seedream-5-lite. All support both generate and edit.

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

python_code.description: Python source to run via "$HOME/openmontage/.venv/bin/python -c" (base64-wrapped server-side, so no shell-quoting risk). cwd is $HOME/openmontage, so `from lib...`/`from tools...` imports resolve. Provide this OR command, not both.

command.description: Raw shell command to run instead of python_code (e.g. a direct ffmpeg/npx call). cwd is $HOME/openmontage. Provide this OR python_code, not both.

secrets.description: Provider secret NAMES to inject into the remote process env for this call (values never reach the agent). Allowlisted to OpenMontage provider shims only.

timeout_seconds.description: Execution ceiling in seconds (default 300, max 540 — SSH exec hard cap is 600s).

note_id.description: Id of the note to read (from list_notes).

title.description: Exact title of the note to read (alternative to note_id).

url.description: Remote https URL of the file to archive. Provide this OR base64.

base64.description: Base64 (or data: URL) of the file bytes. Provide this OR url.

category.description: Archive category. If omitted it is inferred from the MIME type.

title.description: Short human label shown in the archive.

description.description: Longer description of the asset and how to use it.

tags.description: Reusable tags for finding this asset later, e.g. ["b-roll", "city", "night"].

mime_type.description: MIME type, e.g. video/mp4, image/png, text/markdown. Inferred from the source when omitted.

source_tool.description: Which tool/model produced it, e.g. render_remote, abacus_image.

url.description: Remote https URL of the generated media to store (e.g. an image/video/audio link returned by a generation tool). Provide this OR base64.

base64.description: Base64 (or data: URL) of the media bytes. Provide this OR url.

kind.description: Media kind. If omitted it is inferred from the MIME type.

title.description: Short human label shown in the Media gallery card.

prompt.description: The prompt or description that produced this media.

mime_type.description: MIME type, e.g. image/png, video/mp4, audio/mpeg. Inferred from the source when omitted.

source_tool.description: Which tool/model produced it, e.g. voidai_image, chatgpt_image.

engine.description: Which render stack to use. "hyperframes" = an HTML+GSAP composition folder containing index.html (Puppeteer capture → FFmpeg NVENC). "remotion" = a React/TypeScript Remotion project folder.

project_path.description: Absolute local path to the composition folder on the app box. For hyperframes: a folder containing index.html. For remotion: the Remotion project root (containing package.json). node_modules/.git are excluded from upload automatically.

title.description: Human label for the resulting video in the Media gallery. Defaults to the folder name.

duration_seconds.description: HyperFrames only: clip length in seconds (default 5).

width.description: HyperFrames only: viewport width in px (default 1280).

height.description: HyperFrames only: viewport height in px (default 720).

composition_id.description: Remotion only: the composition id to render (default "Main").

entry.description: Remotion only: entry file relative to the project root (e.g. src/index.ts). Auto-detected when omitted.

register.description: Auto-register the resulting MP4 into the Media gallery (Studio › Media). Default true.

content.description: Your complete response to the user's message.

session_id.description: Session ID from the system prompt (if provided).

run_id.description: Run ID from the system prompt (if provided).

skill_name.description: Name of the skill that owns the script (must already exist; create one with manage_skill first).

script.description: Filename of the script inside that skill's scripts/ folder, e.g. "extract.py". Single segment — no path components.

args.description: Argv passed to the script. Each element is one CLI argument; no shell interpolation.

stdin.description: Optional input piped to the script's stdin (capped at 1 MB).

cwd.description: Working directory for the script. Defaults to the script's folder.

timeout_ms.description: Hard timeout in ms. Capped at 2× EXEC_TIMEOUT_MS.

secrets.description: Broker secret names to inject as environment variables for this script, scoped to you. Values are placed directly into the child process and never shown to you; script output is scrubbed of them.

purpose.description: Short reason the secrets are needed — recorded in the broker audit log.

task.description: What the sub-agent should do — be specific and self-contained

context.description: Relevant context the sub-agent needs (max ~2000 tokens). Do NOT paste the full conversation — summarize what matters.

agent_name.description: Optional specialist persona name for the sub-agent

notify_policy.description: Controls when proactive updates are delivered. done_only (default): notify on completion/failure/blocked only. all_updates: also notify on progress heartbeats. never: silent — result queryable only via get_subtask_result.

allow_bash.description: Set true to route this sub-agent through the shell-capable executor (supports git, npm, file writes). Off by default — sub-agents use Kimi K2.6/MiniMax and return output as text.

allow_composio.description: Set true to let this sub-agent call Composio tools (COMPOSIO_*) for READ/lookup/pagination work — e.g. driving a multi-page YouTube/Gmail/Notion list loop without burning the parent context. Off by default. Blast-radius note: this blesses the whole Composio surface for the spawn, so keep destructive actions (delete/send) on the parent for human review and scope the sub-agent task to fetch-only.

kind.description: Optional explicit routing hint. 'code' → Kimi K2.6 (coding model). 'prose' → MiniMax 2.7 (research/planning/writing). Omit to let the triage scorer decide based on task content.

force.description: Bypass the duplicate-task guard and spawn even if an identical subtask recently ran in this session. Only set when the user explicitly wants a re-run.

input.description: Optional context string stored with the run record (not interpolated as $INPUT in workflow nodes)

summary.description: Distilled summary of what happened in the session.

schedule.description: Cron expression e.g. "0 9 * * *". Omit for inbound-webhook-only jobs.

config.description: JSON string with type-specific config. agent_message: {agentId,message,sessionId?}. outbound_webhook: {url,method?,headers?,body?}. shell_command: {command,timeout?}. n8n_workflow: {baseUrl,apiKey,workflowId,payload?}. kestra_flow: {namespace,flowId,inputs?,baseUrl?,apiKey?}

on_complete_webhook_url.description: URL to POST to after each successful run

enable_inbound.description: Generate an inbound webhook slug so external services can trigger this job

query.description: Substring to search across title, description, and tags.
Natural-language query to search the knowledge base.

source.description: Restrict to a single source_id (from list_knowledge_sources).

id.description: The attachment_id of the uploaded document to search — the UUID after "attachment_id:" in the system context block.

query.description: A natural-language question or keywords. Returns the most semantically relevant passages from the document instead of the whole thing — use this for LARGE pre-parsed documents flagged "use search_document" rather than dumping the full markdown into context.
Natural-language query to search the knowledge base.

source.description: Restrict to a single source_id (from list_knowledge_sources).

query.description: Substring to search for in log messages.

service.description: Optional service filter, e.g. "N8N" or "GH" — matched case-insensitively.

path.description: Absolute path to the file on disk. The file must already exist — use fs_write to create it first if needed.

caption.description: Optional message shown above the file card in chat.

filename.description: Override the display name shown to the user. Defaults to the basename of path.

base64.description: Raw base64-encoded image bytes (no data: prefix). Use when you produced the image yourself (e.g. from browserless_screenshot). Provide either this OR url, not both.

url.description: Remote http(s) URL of an existing image to display. Use when referencing an image already hosted elsewhere. Provide either this OR base64, not both.

mime_type.description: MIME type. Required when passing base64. Determines the saved file extension. Defaults to image/png.

alt.description: Short description of the image (used as alt text and rendered as a caption in the markdown image tag). Strongly recommended for accessibility.

caption.description: Optional caption text shown below the image. Plain text — keep it short.

name.description: Unique name for the temporary agent.

systemPrompt.description: Full system prompt for the spawn.

taskDescription.description: The specific task the spawn should execute now.

remote_path.description: Absolute path of the remote file to download.

local_path.description: Destination absolute path on the local host.

machine.description: Registered machine name or id (from the Connect → Machines tab).

command.description: Shell command to run on the remote host.

local_path.description: Absolute path of the local file to upload.

remote_path.description: Destination absolute path on the remote host.

job_id.description: ID of the job to update

prompt.description: Text directions for the edit (e.g. "change the sky to a sunrise", "remove the tree"). Short, descriptive prompts work best.

input_image.description: Required. The image to edit. Accepts: an https URL; a session upload id (from list_uploads); a local file path (absolute, or relative to your workspace, ending in .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp/.gif/.bmp); a base64 string; or a "data:image/...;base64," URI. PREFER the upload id or file path for local images — the server reads the file directly, so the bytes never have to be inlined into your own context (which is slow and can be rejected for large images).

model.description: Venice edit model id. Omit to use Venice's built-in default. Options: firered-image-edit, qwen-edit-uncensored, grok-imagine-edit, grok-imagine-quality-edit, qwen-image-2-edit, qwen-image-2-pro-edit, wan-2-7-pro-edit, flux-2-max-edit, gpt-image-2-edit, gpt-image-1-5-edit, nano-banana-2-edit, nano-banana-pro-edit, nano-banana-2-lite-edit, luma-uni-1-edit, luma-uni-1-max-edit, seedream-v5-lite-edit, seedream-v5-pro-edit, seedream-v4-edit.

aspect_ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. "auto" (or omit) infers the closest supported ratio from the input image; set explicitly when exact output dimensions are required.

resolution.description: Resolution tier, e.g. "1K", "2K", "4K". Supported values vary by model. Default "1K".

output_format.description: Output format. When omitted, inferred from resolution: PNG for 1K, JPEG for 2K/4K.

quality.description: Quality tier — currently only honored by gpt-image-2-edit (default "high" for that model). Ignored by other models.

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate, or the edit instruction to apply to input_image.

operation.description: Default 'generate' (text→image). 'edit' transforms input_image per the prompt and REQUIRES input_image.

input_image.description: For edit: the source image. Accepts an https URL, a session upload id (from list_uploads), a local file path, a base64 string, or a "data:image/...;base64," URI. PREFER the upload id or file path for local images — read server-side, never inlined into your context. Ignored for generate.

resolution.description: Output resolution. STANDARD≈1K (default), plus 2K and 4K. Gemini Pro is the right model for 2K/4K.

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate, or the edit instruction to apply to input_image.

operation.description: Default 'generate' (text→image). 'edit' transforms input_image per the prompt and REQUIRES input_image.

input_image.description: For edit: the source image. Accepts an https URL, a session upload id (from list_uploads), a local file path, a base64 string, or a "data:image/...;base64," URI. PREFER the upload id or file path for local images — read server-side, never inlined into your context. Ignored for generate.

mask.description: For edit: optional mask (https URL or base64 data URL). Transparent areas of the mask mark the region to change (inpaint). Ignored for generate.

size.description: Output size. Default "1024x1024"; 1024x1536 (portrait) and 1536x1024 (landscape) also supported.

model.description: VoidAI gpt-image model id. Default "gpt-image-2". Others: "gpt-image-1.5", "gpt-image-1".

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

prompt.description: Text describing the image to generate, or the edit instruction to apply to input_image.

operation.description: Default 'generate' (text→image). 'edit' transforms input_image per the prompt and REQUIRES input_image.

input_image.description: For edit: the source image. Accepts an https URL, a session upload id (from list_uploads), a local file path, a base64 string, or a "data:image/...;base64," URI. PREFER the upload id or file path for local images — read server-side, never inlined into your context. Ignored for generate.

model.description: VoidAI image model id. Default "gemini-3.1-flash-image" (Nano-Banana). Other Gemini image ids like "gemini-3-pro-image" also work.

alt.description: Alt text for the displayed image. Defaults to the prompt.

caption.description: Optional caption shown below the image.

query.description: Search query. 2-8 keywords work better than full sentences.

category.description: Search category (default general). Use "it" for programming/dev queries, "science" for academic papers, "news" with time_range=day for fresh news.

time_range.description: Restrict results to this recency window.

title.description: Short title for the note (shown in the Notes tab list).

content.description: The note body in Markdown. No length limit — use this to hand the user a long, continuous document instead of a truncated Discord message.

pinned.description: Pin to the top of the Notes list. Default false.

type.description: episodic | semantic | procedural | preference | insight | project | session_summary
